Title: Cytosolic phosphoglucose isomerase is essential for microsporogenesis and embryogenesis in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phosphoglucose isomerase family; cytosolic phosphoglucose isomerase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Callose deposition is essential for intersporal wall formation during microsporogenesis and successful pollen development; carbohydrate partitioning is also required for early embryogenesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: meiocyte intersporal callose wall in anthers
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or strong repression of cPGI impairs intersporal callose wall formation, causes incomplete meiotic cytokinesis, pollen degeneration, male sterility, reduced female fertility, and pre-globular embryo defects; also causes starch-excess leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cPGI deficiency in multiple genetic backgrounds consistently caused failure of intersporal callose wall formation during male meiosis. 2. Strong cPGI repression in floral buds correlated with severity of male sterility, while complete knockout caused complete sterility. 3. cpgi knockout embryos were normally not recoverable under standard conditions, indicating an essential role in early embryogenesis, but some homozygotes could be rescued by sugar feeding. 4. cPGI-YFP driven by 35S rescued lethality but not fertility because expression was undetectable in tapetum and pollen.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; RNAi knockdown; co-suppression lines; transgenic complementation with p35S::cPGI-YFP; in-gel PGI activity assay; immunoblotting; confocal localization; RT-qPCR; aniline blue staining; DAPI staining; TEM; embryo clearing microscopy; pollen viability staining; sugar-feeding rescue
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; relatively high in cotyledons, young leaves, flowers, young anthers, ovules, young seeds, tapetum, microgametogenesis stages, and developing embryos
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively high in stamens, pistils, floral buds, young siliques, tapetum, and developing pollen/embryo stages
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sugar feeding of flowers/pistils with sucrose, glucose, or fructose partially rescued homozygous knockout recovery; plants grown under 16 h light/8 h dark
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome datasets compiled in Arabidopsis eFP Browser; tapetum-specific RNA-seq from Li et al. 2017; accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Diminished young vegetative growth; delayed flowering; reduced female fertility; complete male sterility when strongly repressed or absent
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive, but ineffective in tapetum/pollen and early embryo contexts)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary gene for engineering carbon partitioning: constitutive complementation may fail in reproductive tissues; tissue-specific expression would be required to avoid sterility and embryo lethality
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although cPGI is an enzyme, this study focuses on its broader indispensable role in carbohydrate partitioning rather than direct demonstration of flux to a single terminal nutrient product; authors infer defects are unlikely due to altered expression of callose metabolism genes such as CalS11/12.
